# dropply-web usage guide for AI agents

`dropply-web` is a local-first browser encryption tool with optional sharing.
All encryption and decryption happen **in the tab** (a Web Worker running
`@cdlab/cipher` — XChaCha20-Poly1305; Argon2id password mode or ECIES/secp256k1
public-key mode). Sharing uploads the **finished ciphertext** to `dropply-api`
and returns an 8-char retrieval code; no key material ever appears in a URL,
request, or email. Next.js App Router, **statically exported**
(`output: 'export'`) — no server of its own.

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- HTTP client (all endpoints): ./src/lib/api.ts
- Crypto engine: ./src/workers/cryptoWorker.ts + ./src/hooks/useCryptoProcessor.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web <script>`.

## What it is

- **Static export, no backend here.** `next.config.ts` `output: 'export'` →
  `next build` emits static HTML/JS to `./out`. No SSR, no route handlers, no
  edge/node functions. `src/middleware.ts` (next-intl) and `generateMetadata`
  run at dev/build only. All logic is client-side React.
- **Single tool, no tabs.** `[locale]/page.tsx` is a ScrollArea shell:
  AppHeader (history/retrieve/keys/lang/theme icons) + Hero embedding
  `LocalCryptoPanel` + landing sections. Encrypt vs decrypt is derived from
  the input via `detect()` magic bytes — never add mode tabs back.
- **Sharing is optional.** The tool works fully offline; `dropply-api` is only
  needed for share/retrieve/email. API failure degrades sharing only.

## Crypto engine (workers/cryptoWorker.ts, hooks/useCryptoProcessor.ts)

- One `useCryptoProcessor` instance (created in `page.tsx`) is shared by the
  tool, the header RetrieveEntry, and result cards.
- The worker handles encrypt/decrypt × file/text: `streamCrypto.encrypt/
  decrypt.withPassword|withPublicKey|withPrivateKey`, `textCrypto.*`, with
  chunk progress messages.
- `detect()` on input: `'pwd'`/`'pubk'` → decrypt mode (and presets
  password vs publickey); `'unencrypted'` → encrypt. Key input classified by
  `lib/keys.ts`: mnemonic phrase | 64-hex private key | base58 public key.
- Results are `ProcessResult` rows in `useProcessStore`; ids from
  `@cdlab/driftflake` (`lib/genid.ts`). Original filename lives inside the
  encrypted header and is restored on decrypt.

## Keys (lib/keys.ts, lib/pin.ts, components/keys/)

- Key pairs: BIP39 mnemonic (128-bit) → BIP32 `m/44'/0'/0'/0/0` → secp256k1.
  Private = 32-byte hex; public = base58(compressed 33B).
- `useKeysStore` persists to `localStorage` `dropply-keys` via
  `store/keys-storage.ts` — base64 OBFUSCATION, not encryption. Only mnemonics
  persist; private keys re-derive on demand. Optional PIN = Argon2id hash
  (`saltHex:hashHex`) gating the manager UI only (D2 keystore encryption is
  deferred — do not claim the keystore is encrypted).

## Share — model A (lib/share-blob.ts, LocalResultCard)

Sharing uploads an already-encrypted result as-is. Flow:

1. `getConfig()` → `{ requirePassword, emailShareEnabled, maxFileSize }`
   (fetched at share time, not on mount).
2. Size check → `ShareTooLargeError` before any network write.
3. `createChest(password?)` → `{ sessionId, uploadToken }`. When the server
   sets `SHARE_PASSWORD`, the body must carry `password`; a 401 clears the
   cached password (`useAuthStore`, sessionStorage `dropply-auth`) and
   re-prompts. There is NO dedicated verify endpoint.
4. Upload the blob as **`share.enc` / `application/octet-stream`** — never the
   real filename/MIME (they live inside the encrypted header). Small → direct
   form; >20MB → multipart (20MB parts, `min(3, totalParts)` concurrent,
   `MAX_RETRIES = 3`).
5. `completeUpload(...)` → `{ retrievalCode }` (8-char).
6. Link = `?code=<retrievalCode>` — NEVER put key material in the URL.

Email share (`EmailShare`, only when `emailShareEnabled`): sends the code via
`POST /api/email/share` — the email carries the code only, never a key.

Multipart invariant: the `uploadId` returned by `createMultipartUpload` is
itself a JWT (`multipartToken`), DISTINCT from the chest `uploadToken`.

## Retrieve (components/retrieve/RetrieveEntry.tsx)

Header dialog, no separate screen. `?code=` deep links auto-open it.

1. `retrieveChest(code)` (6–8 char) → `{ files, chestToken }`.
2. Per file `downloadFile(fileId, chestToken)` — `Authorization: Bearer`
   header ONLY (never a `?token=` query).
3. Blobs wrapped as `File`s → `crypto.handleFileSelect` → auto-detect →
   decrypt mode. The user supplies the out-of-band password / private key.

## Client storage (src/store/*, src/lib/storage.ts)

- `useProcessStore` — `localStorage` `dropply-process-results` (completed
  results' metadata only) + IndexedDB `dropply-process-data` (the ArrayBuffer
  payloads). Rehydrate restores blobs + object URLs; a missing blob marks the
  row FAILED ("Data lost"). Removing a row deletes its IDB blob.
- `useKeysStore` — see Keys above.
- `useAuthStore` — `{ sharePassword }` in sessionStorage `dropply-auth`
  (tab-scoped, not durable).
- No backend DB in this app. API type contracts: `src/types/index.ts`.

## Config & endpoints

- Only build-time env: `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (`src/lib/api.ts`, default `''` =
  same-origin relative `/api`, served by nsl in dev — `pnpm dev:dropply` runs
  web + api together at `http://dropply.localhost:3355`). Inlined into the
  static bundle — a change needs a rebuild.
- Runtime config from `GET /api/config` (`requirePassword`,
  `emailShareEnabled`, `maxFileSize`). No rebuild to change server behavior.
- No Cloudflare bindings (no KV/R2/D1/DO). `wrangler.jsonc` serves `./out` as
  static assets only.
- Backend endpoints (all via `PocketChestAPI`, envelope `{ code, message,
  data }`, success `code === 0`): `/api/config`, `/api/chest`,
  `/api/chest/:id/upload`, `/api/chest/:id/multipart/create`,
  `/api/chest/:id/multipart/:fileId/part/:n`,
  `/api/chest/:id/multipart/:fileId/complete`, `/api/chest/:id/complete`,
  `/api/retrieve/:code`, `/api/download/:fileId`, `/api/email/share`.

## Gotchas for agents

- NEVER send or store encryption keys/passwords server-side, put them in URLs,
  or include them in the share email — confidentiality rests on out-of-band
  exchange.
- Uploads must stay opaque: `share.enc` + `application/octet-stream`. Don't
  "improve" sharing by attaching real filenames/MIME to the upload.
- The keystore is base64-obfuscated, NOT encrypted; the PIN gates the UI only.
- Static export: there is NO server here. Don't add API routes, SSR fetching,
  or server components expecting a runtime. `middleware.ts` is inert in prod.
- `nodejs_compat` in `wrangler.jsonc` serves the `next-on-pages` path — do NOT
  assume Node APIs in the client bundle.
- Download auth is the `Authorization: Bearer` header only — never reintroduce
  `?token=`.
- No test script and no test files exist — don't invent `pnpm … test`.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm dev:dropply                             # web + api → http://dropply.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web typecheck   # tsc --noEmit
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web build       # next build → static out/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/dropply-web build:cf    # next-on-pages (Cloudflare Pages)
```
